[[Tariq Sims]] (brother)
[[Ruan Sims]] (sister)
[[Reagan Campbell-Gillard]] (cousin) }} }} '''Ashton Sims''' (born 26 February 1985) is a former [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at club level for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], [[Brisbane Broncos]] and the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL), the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], as a [[Rugby league positions#Forwards|forward]] ==Background== He is an Australian-born [[Fijian nationality law|Fijian]] of [[Fijians|iTaukei]] descent. He was born in [[Sutherland, New South Wales]], located in the [[List of Sydney suburbs|Sydney suburbs]] of [[Australia]]. ==Playing career== While attending [[Kiama High School]] in 2002, Sims was selected to play for the [[Australia national schoolboy rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]].{{Cite web |title=Australian Schoolboys |url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080821104534/http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424 |archive-date=2008-08-21 |access-date=2008-10-10 |website=Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League |publisher=Sporting Pulse}} He played his junior football for the [[Gerringong Lions]].{{Cite news |last=Heming |first=Wayne |date=2011-05-05 |title=Best mates continue NRL rivalry |publisher=[[National Rugby League]] |agency=Australian Associated Press |url=http://www.nrl.com/best-mates-continue-nrl-rivalry/tabid/10874/newsid/62629/default.aspx |url-status=dead |access-date=2011-10-29 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110827175825/http://www.nrl.com/best-mates-continue-nrl-rivalry/tabid/10874/newsid/62629/default.aspx |archive-date=2011-08-27}} ===St. George-Illawarra Dragons=== Sims played for his local NRL club, [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], from 2003 to 2007, making his NRL début against the [[Penrith Panthers]] in round nine of the 2003 season. In 2004 he suffered a suspected broken jaw, having been elbowed in a wayward shoulder charge by Newcastle's [[Clint Newton]]. Newton was sent off and suspended for twelve weeks. Sims however managed to play out the game.{{Cite news |last=Walter |first=Brad |date=2004-08-26 |title=Newton struck out for 12 weeks |work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |url=http://www.smh.com.au/articles/2004/08/25/1093246621615.html |access-date=2011-10-29 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121022142401/http://www.smh.com.au/articles/2004/08/25/1093246621615.html |archive-date=2012-10-22}}{{Cite web |last=Williams |first=Steven |date=2004-08-25 |title=Clint Newton suspended for 12 matches |url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=5446 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120405153124/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=5446 |archive-date=2012-04-05 |access-date=2011-10-29 |website=League Unlimited}} During the 2007 season, the Dragons told Sims he was no longer wanted at the club, and he signed for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] on a three-year contract starting in 2008.{{Cite news |last=Balym |first=Todd |date=2007-06-23 |title=Sims a Bronco, Petero unclear |work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|location=Sydney |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/sims-a-bronco-petero-unclear/story-e6frexnr-1111113809462 |access-date=2011-10-29}} He played 81 NRL matches for the Dragons over five seasons. ===Brisbane Broncos=== [[File:Ashton Sims.JPG|thumb|left|Sims in action for Broncos]] Sims made his [[Brisbane Broncos]] début in the Round 1, 2008 clash against reigning [[wooden spoon (award)|wooden spooner]]s, the [[Penrith Panthers]], in a 48–12 win. Later that year, an elimination semi-final against the [[Melbourne Storm]], Sims started the match in the back-row. The Broncos were leading at the time, but went on to lose the game in the final minute, after Sims dropped the ball with two minutes remaining in the match, which led to the match winning try by [[Greg Inglis]]. Sims described it as "one of the low points" of his career. Sims played almost 60 games for the club and was one of the club's highest paid players, he played in the back-row and in the front row during his time at the Broncos.{{Cite news |last=Heming |first=Wayne |date=2008-10-08 |title=Ashton Sims excited about Fiji selection |work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|location=Sydney |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/ashton-sims-excited-about-fiji-selection/story-e6frexp0-1111117697631 |access-date=2011-10-29}} Shortly after the Broncos' semi-final exit, Sims was named in the [[Fiji national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Training Squad|Fiji]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]]{{Cite web |date=2008-10-08 |title=Anderson cleared for Tonga squad |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081013130431/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm |archive-date=2008-10-13 |access-date=2008-10-10 |publisher=BBC Sport }} and was later confirmed for the final squad. He had only visited Fiji once in his life—for a holiday—but qualified to play for the country because his mother was born there. He was Fiji's starting second-rower for all four of their matches; Fiji qualified for the tournament's semi-final for the first time.{{Cite web |title=Ashton Sims – Matches played for Fiji |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/NjUtLS0tLS0tLTEzNTctLS0tLXktLS0t |access-date=2009-06-28 |publisher=Rugby League Project}} Sims fell out of the Broncos' starting line-up early in the 2009 season, finding himself playing for the [[Norths Devils]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].{{Cite news |last=Ricketts |first=Steve |date=2009-04-21 |title=Forward Ashton Sims could quit Brisbane Broncos |work=[[The Courier-Mail]] |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/sims-could-quit-broncos/story-e6frep5o-1225700390761 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20120729204733/http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/sims-could-quit-broncos/story-e6frep5o-1225700390761 |url-status=dead |archive-date=29 July 2012 |access-date=2011-10-29}} He was recalled to the Broncos' first thirteen for the team's finals campaign, in which they fell one match short of qualifying for the Grand Final.{{Cite news |last=Heming |first=Wayne |date=2009-09-15 |title=Sims earns Broncos return |work=[[The Courier-Mail]] |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/sims-earns-broncos-return/story-e6frep5x-1225773803743 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20120723221710/http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/sims-earns-broncos-return/story-e6frep5x-1225773803743 |url-status=dead |archive-date=23 July 2012 |access-date=2011-10-29}} However, he only played nine NRL matches in the following season, and in August signed to play for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in 2011.{{Cite news |last=Koch |first=Dan |last2=McDonald |first2=Margie |date=2010-08-03 |title=Titans escort Greg Inglis on full tour of Gold Coast before making $1m offer this week |work=[[The Australian]] |url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/news/sport/titans-escort-greg-inglis-on-full-tour-of-gold-coast-before-making-1m-offer-this-week/story-e6frg7mf-1225900248589 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121231033031/http://www.theaustralian.com.au/news/sport/titans-escort-greg-inglis-on-full-tour-of-gold-coast-before-making-1m-offer-this-week/story-e6frg7mf-1225900248589 |url-status=dead |archive-date=31 December 2012 |access-date=2011-10-29}} ===North Queensland Cowboys=== [[File:Ashton Sims Warrington.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Sims in action for Warrington]] Sims moved to North Queensland with his younger brother, [[Tariq Sims|Tariq]], who had played in the Broncos' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Under-20s]] team.{{cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2011-01-28/cowboys-hurt-by-criticism-thurston/1921912|title=Cowboys hurt by criticism: Thurston|date=28 January 2011|publisher=[[ABC News (Australia)|ABC News]]|location=Australia|access-date=29 October 2011}} He played in 24 matches for the Cowboys in the 2011 season, and was a substitute for their qualifying final loss to [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]]. Ashton Sims led the way for NRL props in [[offload]]s in 2011, making 42 to his teammates. Sims hit career best form for the Cowboys in 2012 averaging 108 metres and 22 tackles. He was in the Cowboys starting pack while rep star Matthew Scott was out with a foot injury.{{cn|date=June 2025}} On 11 May 2012, it was announced that the oldest Sims brother had re-signed with the club for a further two seasons, which saw him at the club until the end of the [[2014 NRL season]].{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/statistics|title=2011 Season Statistics|work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|location=Sydney|access-date=31 October 2011}}{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ni04MTktNC0tLS0tLTEzNTctLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Ashton Sims: NRL 2011 match list|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=29 October 2011}} ===Warrington Wolves=== Sims signed for Warrington Wolves ready for the 'New Era' of Super League in 2015, with a reformatted competition. Whilst at Warrington Wolves he received a League Leaders Winners medal and Runners up Medals for the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]]{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}} and the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|work=The Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}} ===Toronto Wolfpack=== Sims signed for Toronto Wolfpack following the 2017 Super League season. The Canadian side played in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] in 2018 following their [[RFL League 1|League 1]] title in their inaugural season in 2017. ==Representative career== Ashton represented Fiji in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]]. He appeared in all 5 of Fiji's matches. In May 2014, Ashton captained Fiji in the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations#Samoa v. Fiji|2014 Pacific Test]] against {{rlnt|Samoa}}. Fiji failed to win the test match and in doing so, failed to qualify for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]]. In October 2017, Ashton represented Fiji once again in the World Cup, held in his home country Australia. There, Fiji beat New Zealand in the Quarter Finals but unfortunately suffered a loss in the semifinals to Australia and cousin – Reagan Campbell-Gillard. ==Personal life== Sims is the older brother of fellow rugby league players [[Korbin Sims|Korbin]] and [[Tariq Sims]]. His elder sister [[Ruan Sims]] played for the [[Australian Jillaroos|Australian women's team]] in 2010.{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/sims-sister-ruan-leading-way-as-female-participation-swells-20130517-2jrkx.html|title=Sims sister Ruan leading way as female participation swells|author=Brad Walter (The Sydney Morning Herald)|date=17 May 2013|access-date=16 July 2014}} He is married to Nicole Sims and father of 4 children. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[https://www.torontowolfpack.com/player/ashton-sims/ Toronto Wolfpack profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20150804090724/http://warringtonwolves.com/team-player-22 Profile at warringtonwolves.com] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20171105170435/http://www.rlwc2017.com/players?team=382#Ashton-Sims Statistics at rlwc2017.com] {{Toronto Wolfpack -
